zh.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,是Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara和Teresa Díaz II de Haro的女兒，於1285年出生在西班牙安達盧希亞科爾多瓦蒙塔爾巴 德 科爾多瓦拉臘 德 洛斯 音法斯。她於大約1299年與Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador結婚。她去世於1351年12月12日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞享年66歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Jorge, Luis, Pedro, Jesus, Roberto, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish: habitational name from a place called Lara de los Infantes in Burgos province. Compare   De Lara  .

variants.name: De Lara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Juan, Jose, Luis, Mauricio, Pedro, Alfonso, Ana, Jaime, Javier, Jorge, Merlinda.
Spanish: variant, with the preposition de ‘from’, of Lara .

nameMeanings.name: Alvarez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Ramon, Pedro, Miguel, Roberto.  Portuguese Catarina, Lidio, Ligia, Joaquim, Wenceslao.
Spanish (Álvarez): patronymic from the personal name Álvaro (see Alvaro ). Compare Alavarez , Albares , Albarez , Alvares , and Alverez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Alverez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Alberto, Alfredo, Francisco, Mario, Miguel, Ramon, Carlos, Javier, Luis, Manuel, Marcelino.
Hispanic (Álverez): altered form of Spanish Álvarez (see Alvarez ).

variants.name: Albares
meanings: Spanish: variant of Álvarez (see Alvarez ).

variants.name: Alavarez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Álvarez (see Alvarez ).

variants.name: Alvaro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Raul, Adelaida, Alba, Alberto, Alejandro, Anaya, Benito, Celestino, Chavez, Emilia.  Italian Antonio, Alfonse, Angelo, Cesare, Giuseppe, Rocco, Sal.
Spanish and Portuguese (Álvaro); Italian: from the personal name Álvaro, Alvaro, which is of ancient Germanic (Visigothic) origin, but the exact etymology is not clear.

variants.name: Albarez
meanings: Spanish (Álbarez): variant of Álvarez (see Alvarez ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Juana
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of   Juan .  It is now also occasionally used in the English-speaking world, to which it was introduced mainly by Hispanic settlers in the United States.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Nunez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Rafael, Pedro, Jorge, Ramon.  Portuguese Guilherme, Joaquim, Vasco, Wenceslao.
Spanish (Núñez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Nuño (see Nuno ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Nuno
meanings: Anglicized form of the Irish name Úna , assimilated to the name of the Roman goddess Juno, consort of Jupiter.

nameMeanings.name: "la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of   Olaf  , also widely used in southwest Sweden.

en.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, was born in 1285, in Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalusia, Spain as the daughter of Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara and Teresa Díaz II de Haro. She married Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador about 1299. She died on 12 December 1351, in Palencia, Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, est née en 1285, à Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, Espagne, fille de Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara et Teresa Díaz II de Haro. Elle a épousé Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador environ 1299. Elle est décédée le 12 décembre 1351, à Palencia, Castilla y León, Espagne, à l’âge de 66 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, è nata il nel 1285 nel Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, Spagna come figlia di Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara e Teresa Díaz II de Haro. Lei sposato Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador circa 1299. Morì il 12 dicembre 1351, nel Palencia, Castilla y León, Spagna, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,は1285年にスペインアンダルクiアクoルドバモンタルブaン ド クoルドバラーア ド ロス インファンツでJuan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de LaraとTeresa Díaz II de Haroの娘として生まれた。約1299年にInfante Enrique de Castilla el Senadorと結婚した。彼女は1351年12月12日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンプアレンシアで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera,(은)는 1285년 스페인 앤다루ci아 co르도바 몽알브a 데 co르도바 라라 데 로스 인팡츠에서 Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara(와)과 Teresa Díaz II de Haro의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1299년 Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1351년 12월 12일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 파렌샤에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, nasceu em 1285, em Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andaluzia, Espanha filha de Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara e Teresa Díaz II de Haro. Ela casou-se com Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador aproximadamente 1299. Ela faleceu em 12 de dezembro de 1351, em Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha, com 66 anos.

ru.Lerma, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain: лирма, бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, родилась в 1285 г. в местности лара де лос инфантесь, монталбaн де кoрдоба, кoрдоба, андалукiа, Испания. Её родителей звали Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara и Teresa Díaz II de Haro. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador около 1299 г. Она умерла 12 декабря 1351 г. в местности пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Juana Nunez "la palomilla" de Lara y Alvarez de Azagra Señora de Lerma, Villafranca, Dueñas, Fuente-Empudia, Torrelobatón and Herrera, nació en 1285, en Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, España como hija de Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara y Teresa Díaz II de Haro. Se casó con Infante Enrique de Castilla el Senador aproximadamente 1299. Murió el 12 de diciembre de 1351, en Palencia, Castilla y León, España, a la edad de 66 años.
